failedThresholds: Aura3D: hover-highlight behavior is not documented in notes.md and is only described as prefab metadata in source comments.
Three.js: hover-highlight behavior is implemented and visible in source/HUD, but not documented in notes.md.

summary: Three.js wins 4 prompts and Aura3D wins 1. Aura3D consistently uses much less code and reaches render faster, but Three.js more often provides explicit, inspectable behavior and stronger visual evidence for interaction-heavy prompts. Aura3D wins all five prompt comparisons by aggregate metrics, mainly through lower source complexity and faster usable render times. Three.js often has smaller bundles and is visually strongest on the mini-golf and city prompts, but it does not overcome Aura3D's modifiability advantage in this scoring set.
